channel.title: Workday Scraper - RSS Feed
channel.description: An RSS feed for new Workday postings.

item.title: Manulife: 消保岗
item.description:
locations
Shanghai
time type
Full time
posted on
Posted Today
time left to apply
End Date: April 28, 2025 (8 days left to apply)
job requisition id
JR25010001
3-5 sentence summary about the role considering these questions: what is the opportunity? How is this role contributing to our mission? What will they get from this opportunity?
Position Responsibilities:
Xx
Xx
Xx
Xx
Xx
Required Qualifications:
[Years of experience]
[Quantified years of experience with tools/software/etc.]
[Education if required]
[Licenses if required]
Preferred Qualifications:
[Xx]
[Xx]
[Xx]
[Xx]
[Xx]
When you join our team:
We’ll empower you to learn and grow the career you want.
We’ll recognize and support you in a flexible environment where well-being and inclusion are more than just words.
As part of our global team, we’ll support you in shaping the future you want to see.
About Manulife and John Hancock
Manulife Financial Corporation is a leading international financial services provider, helping people make their decisions easier and lives better. To learn more about us, visit https://www.manulife.com/en/about/our-story.html.
Manulife is an Equal Opportunity Employer
At Manulife/John Hancock, we embrace our diversity. We strive to attract, develop and retain a workforce that is as diverse as the customers we serve and to foster an inclusive work environment that embraces the strength of cultures and individuals. We are committed to fair recruitment, retention, advancement and compensation, and we administer all of our practices and programs without discrimination on the basis of race, ancestry, place of origin, colour, ethnic origin, citizenship, religion or religious beliefs, creed, sex (including pregnancy and pregnancy-related conditions), sexual orientation, genetic characteristics, veteran status, gender identity, gender expression, age, marital status, family status, disability, or any other ground protected by applicable law.
It is our priority to remove barriers to provide equal access to employment. A Human Resources representative will work with applicants who request a reasonable accommodation during the application process. All information shared during the accommodation request process will be stored and used in a manner that is consistent with applicable laws and Manulife/John Hancock policies. To request a reasonable accommodation in the application process, contact recruitment@manulife.com.
Working Arrangement
Hybrid

item.title: Manulife: HK Claims Associate
item.description:
locations
Chengdu, Sichuan
time type
Full time
posted on
Posted Today
time left to apply
End Date: April 30, 2025 (10 days left to apply)
job requisition id
JR25040978
The opportunity

4-5 sentence summary of role and day to day tasks.

Responsibilities
Responsibility 1
Responsibility 2
Responsibility 3
Responsibility 4
Responsibility 5

How will you create impact?

2-3 sentences on who do they report into and how does this role affect our org.

What motivates you?
You obsess about customers, listen, engage and act for their benefit.
You think big, with curiosity to discover ways to use your agile approach and enable business outcomes.
You thrive in teams and enjoy getting things done together.
You take ownership and build solutions, focusing on what matters.
You do what is right, work with integrity and speak up.
You share your humanity, helping us build a diverse and inclusive work environment for everyone.

What we are looking for
Requirement 1
Requirement 2
Requirement 3
Requirement 4
Requirement 5

What can we offer you?
A competitive salary and benefits packages.
A growth trajectory that extends upward and outward, encouraging you to follow your passions and learn new skills.
A focus on growing your career path with us.
Flexible work policies and strong work-life balance.
Professional development and leadership opportunities.

Our commitment to you
Values-first culture
We lead with our Values every day and bring them to life together.
Boundless opportunity
We create opportunities to learn and grow at every stage of your career.
Continuous innovation
We invite you to help redefine the future of financial services.
Delivering the promise of Diversity, Equity and Inclusion
We foster an inclusive workplace where everyone thrives.
Championing Corporate Citizenship
We build a business that benefits all stakeholders and has a positive social and environmental impact.
About Manulife and John Hancock
Manulife Financial Corporation is a leading international financial services provider, helping people make their decisions easier and lives better. To learn more about us, visit https://www.manulife.com/en/about/our-story.html.
Manulife is an Equal Opportunity Employer
At Manulife/John Hancock, we embrace our diversity. We strive to attract, develop and retain a workforce that is as diverse as the customers we serve and to foster an inclusive work environment that embraces the strength of cultures and individuals. We are committed to fair recruitment, retention, advancement and compensation, and we administer all of our practices and programs without discrimination on the basis of race, ancestry, place of origin, colour, ethnic origin, citizenship, religion or religious beliefs, creed, sex (including pregnancy and pregnancy-related conditions), sexual orientation, genetic characteristics, veteran status, gender identity, gender expression, age, marital status, family status, disability, or any other ground protected by applicable law.
It is our priority to remove barriers to provide equal access to employment. A Human Resources representative will work with applicants who request a reasonable accommodation during the application process. All information shared during the accommodation request process will be stored and used in a manner that is consistent with applicable laws and Manulife/John Hancock policies. To request a reasonable accommodation in the application process, contact recruitment@manulife.com.
Working Arrangement
Hybrid

item.title: Manulife: Bancassurance Sales Specialist
item.description:
locations
Ninh Kieu Dist., Cần Thơ
time type
Full time
posted on
Posted Today
time left to apply
End Date: May 30, 2025 (30+ days left to apply)
job requisition id
JR25040900
The opportunity
The Sales Manager (SM) is responsible for business planning and strategy execution, sales support and Coaching with bank partners and customers.

Responsibilities
Sales quality management
Building effective cooperative relationships with partners
Training knowledge and professional skills for the bank sales force and IOIS
Managing and implementing effective sales activities
Reporting on business activities and arising issues
Effective internal interaction
Other tasks from the leadership...
What motivates you?
You obsess about customers, listen, engage and act for their benefit.
You think big, with curiosity to discover ways to use your agile approach and enable business outcomes.
You thrive in teams and enjoy getting things done together.
You take ownership and build solutions, focusing on what matters.
You do what is right, work with integrity and speak up.
You share your humanity, helping us build a diverse and inclusive work environment for everyone.

What we are looking for
Bachelor’s degree majoring in marketing, Finance and Banking.
Minimum 2 years sales management experience in insurance or banking businesses.·
Have a good bancassurance background is preferable.
Nice to Have:
Dynamic personality with good ability to interact with different people and level.
Systematic and structured to understand systems and processes.
Strong leadership and communication skills.

What can we offer you?
A competitive salary and benefits packages.
A growth trajectory that extends upward and outward, encouraging you to follow your passions and learn new skills.
A focus on growing your career path with us.
Flexible work policies and strong work-life balance.
Professional development and leadership opportunities.

Our commitment to you
Values-first culture
We lead with our Values every day and bring them to life together.
Boundless opportunity
We create opportunities to learn and grow at every stage of your career.
Continuous innovation
We invite you to help redefine the future of financial services.
Delivering the promise of Diversity, Equity and Inclusion
We foster an inclusive workplace where everyone thrives.
Championing Corporate Citizenship
We build a business that benefits all stakeholders and has a positive social and environmental impact.
About Manulife and John Hancock
Manulife Financial Corporation is a leading international financial services provider, helping people make their decisions easier and lives better. To learn more about us, visit https://www.manulife.com/en/about/our-story.html.
Manulife is an Equal Opportunity Employer
At Manulife/John Hancock, we embrace our diversity. We strive to attract, develop and retain a workforce that is as diverse as the customers we serve and to foster an inclusive work environment that embraces the strength of cultures and individuals. We are committed to fair recruitment, retention, advancement and compensation, and we administer all of our practices and programs without discrimination on the basis of race, ancestry, place of origin, colour, ethnic origin, citizenship, religion or religious beliefs, creed, sex (including pregnancy and pregnancy-related conditions), sexual orientation, genetic characteristics, veteran status, gender identity, gender expression, age, marital status, family status, disability, or any other ground protected by applicable law.
It is our priority to remove barriers to provide equal access to employment. A Human Resources representative will work with applicants who request a reasonable accommodation during the application process. All information shared during the accommodation request process will be stored and used in a manner that is consistent with applicable laws and Manulife/John Hancock policies. To request a reasonable accommodation in the application process, contact recruitment@manulife.com.
Working Arrangement
In Office

item.title: Manulife: Brand Manager
item.description:
locations
Dist.7, Ho Chi Minh
time type
Full time
posted on
Posted Today
time left to apply
End Date: May 30, 2025 (30+ days left to apply)
job requisition id
JR25040644
The opportunity

We are looking for a qualified Branding Manager to join our team to manage Manulife branding activities and drive the success of key cross-functional initiatives.
Responsibilities
Strategic planning and concept development for key brand campaigns/product launching and activities in the year
Manage cross-functional initiatives that bring positive impacts on brand and business performance
Manage consistent brand communications across all public and customer channels
Monitor and analyze brand performance metrics (KPIs) to identify opportunities for improvement and ensure alignment with business objectives.
Manage vendor resources and business-as-usual activities of the brand department
Manage UX/UI projects to enhance user experience across all digital platforms, ensuring consistency with brand identity.
Partner with external agencies and vendors to optimize brand positioning and marketing efforts.

How will you create impact?
Driving consistent brand images and messages across all channels and audiences.
Maintain Manulife's leading brand positions in Vietnam life insurance market across the brand funnel.
Brand influential activities among our internal and external stakeholders, among both distribution force, customers, and prospects.

What motivates you?
You obsess about customers, listen, engage and act for their benefit.
You think big, with curiosity to discover ways to use your agile approach and enable business outcomes.
You thrive in teams and enjoy getting things done together.
You take ownership and build solutions, focusing on what matters.
You do what is right, work with integrity and speak up.
You share your humanity, helping us build a diverse and inclusive work environment for everyone.

What we are looking for
Our ideal candidate has a strategic mindset, good collaboration, and management skills as well as brand management experience. A competitive candidate for this position also possesses:
Good communication skills
Ability to drive creative concepts and engaging content.
Proven experience in social media, communication, and promotional events…
Experience in UXUI is a plus
A customer-oriented mindset with great customer service skills
You should be able to act as the face and voice of our brand in any communications and exposure to all stakeholders and target audiences, from employees, agents, customers, partners, and the public.

What can we offer you?
A competitive salary and benefits package.
A growth trajectory that extends upward and outward, encouraging you to follow your passions and learn new skills.
A focus on growing your career path with us.
Flexible work policies and strong work-life balance.
Professional development and leadership opportunities.

Our commitment to you
Values-first culture
We lead with our Values every day and bring them to life together.
Boundless opportunity
We create opportunities to learn and grow at every stage of your career.
Continuous innovation
We invite you to help redefine the future of financial services.
Delivering the promise of Diversity, Equity and Inclusion
We foster an inclusive workplace where everyone thrives.
Championing Corporate Citizenship
We build a business that benefits all stakeholders and has a positive social and environmental impact.
About Manulife and John Hancock
Manulife Financial Corporation is a leading international financial services provider, helping people make their decisions easier and lives better. To learn more about us, visit https://www.manulife.com/en/about/our-story.html.
Manulife is an Equal Opportunity Employer
At Manulife/John Hancock, we embrace our diversity. We strive to attract, develop and retain a workforce that is as diverse as the customers we serve and to foster an inclusive work environment that embraces the strength of cultures and individuals. We are committed to fair recruitment, retention, advancement and compensation, and we administer all of our practices and programs without discrimination on the basis of race, ancestry, place of origin, colour, ethnic origin, citizenship, religion or religious beliefs, creed, sex (including pregnancy and pregnancy-related conditions), sexual orientation, genetic characteristics, veteran status, gender identity, gender expression, age, marital status, family status, disability, or any other ground protected by applicable law.
It is our priority to remove barriers to provide equal access to employment. A Human Resources representative will work with applicants who request a reasonable accommodation during the application process. All information shared during the accommodation request process will be stored and used in a manner that is consistent with applicable laws and Manulife/John Hancock policies. To request a reasonable accommodation in the application process, contact recruitment@manulife.com.
Working Arrangement
Hybrid

item.title: Manulife: Manager – VTB Recruitment and Business Support and Management
item.description:
locations
Lancaster Luminaire Building, Hanoi
time type
Full time
posted on
Posted Today
time left to apply
End Date: May 30, 2025 (30+ days left to apply)
job requisition id
JR25040568
DUTIES & RESPONSIBILITIES
PARTNER MANAGEMENT
Coordinate with internal & external stakeholders to support Banca Head in the areas of services, operations, product, projects, new initiatives to drive the business development strategies and execution per agreed plans with the bank.
To coordinate with Strategy team on the Annual Strategies Plans, ad-hoc plans with the Partner, mutually lead the execution of those strategies internal and external, ensure achieving good business results.
Assist Banca Head in obtaining market intelligence information to formulate strategies.
Review business processes and implement new initiatives to enhance productivity and efficiency for the partnership.
Build strong relationships with partner’s business functions on aiming to activate, build up and maintain a fruitful business relationship with the Partner in solid manner.
To execute the protocol and meetings with the partner in BAU activities, in coordination with strategy to operate Working Committees, Co-working team meetings with partner and internal in regular timeline.
Supervise Banca Sales Admin to support internal sales activities:
Quarterly and monthly reporting on sales activities plans, promotion/demotion/MOC, sales structures, plan of business trip for SM+, other consolidations for sales team to work with internal departments, etc
Monthly manage other administrative tasks: air tickets, taxi card, accommodations arrangement for SM+ trip, etc.
Daily proceed payments, invoices, clear cash advance, monthly accrual budget for SM+
Other duties assigned by the Head of Bancassurance
RECRUITMENT
In charge of the entire recruitment life cycle of sourcing, identification, interview preparation and closing process for Partnership Distribution (PD) Sales Channels
Act as strategic contact point for assigned projects, perform analysis of hiring needs and provide hiring forecast strategies of Sales Channel, drive and support effective staffing to meet business requirements.
Monitor recruitment plan and keep track, verify recruitment requests from sales team.
Design, plan and execute sourcing & employer branding activities (referral programs, job sites, career fair, BOP, recruitment campaigns)
Work strongly with related department to optimize the selection, onboarding process & improve candidate experience.
Suggest measures for improving employee retention, coordinate with training to improve sales capacity & engagement.
Oversee recruitment team to work and collaborate effectively, hands-on support and guide the team on daily issues and supervise performance.
Accountable for key reports/metrics related to headcount, hiring, attrition, budget.
Ad-hoc projects/ assignments/ tasks required from Head of Bancassurance
C. JOB SPECIFICATION
EDUCATION / TRAINING
A good bachelor’s degree, majoring in Marketing, Business Administration, Economics, Commerce…
Language proficiency: Communication
Fluently in English (both verbal and written).
2. PROFESSIONAL / TRADE QUALIFICATION
Preferably obtain some basic life insurance qualifications
Bachelor in HR or related fields
3. COMPETENCIES
Dynamic personality with good ability to interact with different people and levels Strategic and management skills.
Strong communication skills with ability to communicate confidently at all levels.
Strong people skills, leadership skills.
Strong organization skills, creative and proactive in business initiatives and ideas.
Strong business acumen and problem-solving skills
Analytics, planning & reporting skills
Quick adaptability, enthusiasm, sense of responsibility, good at business partnering mindset.
Ability to work under high pressure.
High degree of self-confidence, proactive, can-do spirit for improvement & result-oriented
4. EXPERIENCE
Work in Insurance / or Banking Industry/or Financial Markets for at least 5 years.
Relationship Management, Account Management, Operation, Sales and Services Management experience
At least 3 years of full cycle of Recruitment experience with proven track record in Services/Banking/Insurance or Finance industry
Proficient in recruitment best practices with organizations with high demands of recruitment
5. COMPUTER PROFICIENCY
Reasonable knowledge of Microsoft Excel, PowerPoint, Word and other designing software
6. KEY WORKING RELATIONSHIP
Internal:
Within Sales - PD departments and with other departments, namely Actuarial, IT, Finance, Legal and Compliance, Corp Services, Marketing, New Business, Customer Service, Claim etc.
External:
Bank Partner/ Other business partners.
Potential new business partners
Recruitment partners/ vendors
About Manulife and John Hancock
Manulife Financial Corporation is a leading international financial services provider, helping people make their decisions easier and lives better. To learn more about us, visit https://www.manulife.com/en/about/our-story.html.
Manulife is an Equal Opportunity Employer
At Manulife/John Hancock, we embrace our diversity. We strive to attract, develop and retain a workforce that is as diverse as the customers we serve and to foster an inclusive work environment that embraces the strength of cultures and individuals. We are committed to fair recruitment, retention, advancement and compensation, and we administer all of our practices and programs without discrimination on the basis of race, ancestry, place of origin, colour, ethnic origin, citizenship, religion or religious beliefs, creed, sex (including pregnancy and pregnancy-related conditions), sexual orientation, genetic characteristics, veteran status, gender identity, gender expression, age, marital status, family status, disability, or any other ground protected by applicable law.
It is our priority to remove barriers to provide equal access to employment. A Human Resources representative will work with applicants who request a reasonable accommodation during the application process. All information shared during the accommodation request process will be stored and used in a manner that is consistent with applicable laws and Manulife/John Hancock policies. To request a reasonable accommodation in the application process, contact recruitment@manulife.com.
Working Arrangement
In Office

item.title: Manulife: コンプライアンス推進部マネジャ―
item.description:
locations
Tokyo
time type
Full time
posted on
Posted Today
time left to apply
End Date: May 30, 2025 (30+ days left to apply)
job requisition id
JR25010394
雇用機会
お客様は私たちにとって最も重要であり、多くのエンドユーザーは私たちの製品・サービスを信頼して安心した日々をおくることが出来ています。私たちのビジネスの将来のために、重大な問題を解決して価値を提供することが出来るコンプライアンス業務推進マネジャ―を求めています。あなたを必要としています。
The Opportunity
The customer is the focus of everything we do, and millions of end users rely on our products daily. We believe in the value of empowering Compliance Manager with the resources to solve critical problems for the future of our business, which is why we need you.
職務詳細：
（過去のご経験・適正に応じて担当いただきます）
コンプライアンス態勢整備、高度化の企画推進
リスク評価・課題認識を行った上での態勢向上のための施策の設計、及びその効果測定
ビジネス推進上のコンプライアンス関連の論点整理、各関連部署に対する助言
コンプライアンス関連の社内啓蒙施策・研修の企画・設計・運営
コンプライアンス的要素を軸とした、全社的課題の認識及び改善案の企画推進および営業関連部署、オペレーション関連部署等、様々な関連部署との連携
募集関連資料および研修資料等のコンプライアンス審査・管理
上記に関連する事項
募集要項：
以下のいずれかを満たす方：
金融機関における法務・コンプライアンス業務経験者もしくは業務企画経験者
金融機関における業務等を通してプレゼンテーション能力の高い方
コンサルティングファーム等で金融事業のコンプライアンスもしくは業務企画・改善関連のプロジェクト経験者
有れば尚可の要件：
Microsoft Access等を活用したデータ分析スキル
言語スキル：
日本語　ネイティブ（必須）
ビジネスレベルの英語力があれば、なお可
人物像
論理的思考力
粘り強く課題を解決していくことへの意欲
プロセス設計・改善への意欲
成長を続ける当社においてコンプライアンス部門の役割は非常に大きくなっています。狭義のコンプライアンスに限定されずに態勢整備・業務企画へ積極柔軟なスタンスで貢献を提供するやり甲斐の大きな業務です。
コンプライアンス部門自体も絶えずの成長を志向することから、部門目標の共有と共にメンバーの自律や裁量のある運営を行います。それらを通じて会社への貢献を実感できることを目指します。
当部門においては、フレックスタイムや在宅勤務制度も積極的に活用した業務推進を行っており、ワークライフバランス実現の観点でも充実した環境となっています。
社外勤務についての部内ガイドライン：
当初は初台オフィス内で部内でのコミュニケーションをとりながら職務を遂行頂き、その後は、週1～2回程度の社外勤務が可能です。
また、部門として在宅勤務・フルフレックスタイム制度の積極的活用を進めています。
残業について：
残業は月20～30時間程度を想定します。
給与：
経験、能力を考慮の上、当社規定により優遇します。
通勤交通費支給（当社規定による）
変動賞与年1回（個人、会社業績に応じて支給）
勤務時間：
原則　9：00～17：00（実働7時間。フレックス制度あり）
福利厚生：
社会保険完備（健康保険、厚生年金保険、雇用保険、労災保険）、ベネフィットワン、各種研修制度(英語、日本語研修含む)、育児・介護休業制度、ベビーシッター支援制度、wiwiwシステム（育児休業からの復職支援システム）の導入、退職金制度
休日休暇：
完全週休2日制（土・日）、祝日、年末年始、有給休暇、特別連続休暇、アニバーサリー休暇他
勤務地：
初台本社　〒163-1430　東京都新宿区西新宿三丁目20-2　東京オペラシティタワー30階
あなたを動機づけは何ですか？
•あなたはお客様に一生懸命になり、お客様の利益のために耳を傾け、関与し、行動します
•あなたはアジャイルなマインドセットでビジネスの成果を実現する方法を見つけたいという好奇心を持って、広い視野で考えます
•あなたはチームで成長し、一緒に物事を成し遂げることを楽しみます
•あなたは責任感をもって、重要事項に対するソリューションを構築します
•あなたは正しいことをし、誠実に働き、率直に発言します
•あなたの人間性を共有し、多様で包括的な職場環境を構築するのを助けます
私たちのコミットメント
我々の使命;　意思決定をより簡単にし、より良い生活を送るために貢献すること
あなたの成長と成功のためのリーダーシップチームであること
業界の変革を推進するリーダーとなるという大胆な目標
毎日最善を尽くす
jobs.manulife.comで私たちの雇用機会についてもっと学んでください
What motivates you?
You obsess about customers, listen, engage and act for their benefit
You think big, with curiosity to discover ways to use your agile mindset and enable business outcomes
You thrive in teams, and enjoy getting things done together
You take ownership and build solutions, focusing on what matters
You do what is right, work with integrity and speak up
You share your humanity, helping us build a diverse and inclusive work environment for everyone
Our commitment to you
Our mission; to be a part of making Decisions Easier and Lives Better
A leadership team dedicated to your growth and success
A bold ambition and set of goals to be a leader in driving transformation in our industry
Our best. Every day.
Learn more about opportunities with us at jobs.manulife.com
About Manulife and John Hancock
Manulife Financial Corporation is a leading international financial services provider, helping people make their decisions easier and lives better. To learn more about us, visit https://www.manulife.com/en/about/our-story.html.
Manulife is an Equal Opportunity Employer
At Manulife/John Hancock, we embrace our diversity. We strive to attract, develop and retain a workforce that is as diverse as the customers we serve and to foster an inclusive work environment that embraces the strength of cultures and individuals. We are committed to fair recruitment, retention, advancement and compensation, and we administer all of our practices and programs without discrimination on the basis of race, ancestry, place of origin, colour, ethnic origin, citizenship, religion or religious beliefs, creed, sex (including pregnancy and pregnancy-related conditions), sexual orientation, genetic characteristics, veteran status, gender identity, gender expression, age, marital status, family status, disability, or any other ground protected by applicable law.
It is our priority to remove barriers to provide equal access to employment. A Human Resources representative will work with applicants who request a reasonable accommodation during the application process. All information shared during the accommodation request process will be stored and used in a manner that is consistent with applicable laws and Manulife/John Hancock policies. To request a reasonable accommodation in the application process, contact recruitment@manulife.com.
Working Arrangement
Hybrid

item.title: Manulife: HR Operation Manager
item.description:
locations
Hatsudai Head Office
time type
Full time
posted on
Posted Today
time left to apply
End Date: May 31, 2025 (30+ days left to apply)
job requisition id
JR24121411
The Opportunity
The customer is the focus of everything we do, and millions of end users rely on our products daily. We believe in the value of empowering Payroll Manager with the resources to solve critical problems for the future of our business, which is why we need you.
Role Summary:
This position is responsible in managing, directing, and overseeing the end-to-end Payroll operations. In additional to the BAU payroll operations, this position is also responsible in making sure all payroll related projects are delivered and executed in a timely manner. The Payroll Manager is also accountable in ensuring the integrity of internal controls through the execution of an accurate and timely payroll. The Payroll Manager is expected to equip the direct reports and other team members with skills and knowledge required in the job, monitoring and managing team’s performance.
Job Description:
Operations Management
Lead a team who is responsible in the day-to-day operations as well as the payroll processing of 2,000 plus employees.
Responsible in processing off-cycle payment as well as the periodic bonus.
Manage the completion of the periodic payroll and other Adhoc reports as well as the preparation of the year-end compliance requirement with the tax and statutory agencies.
Ensure compliance with all regulatory reporting requirements, guidelines, and adherence with all internal policies and internal controls.
Maintain knowledge of existing & new legislations which affects the payroll calculations and enforcing adherence to the requirements and advising management on the needed actions
Oversee daily processing activities, including allocating and monitoring workflow to ensure the resources are fully maximized.
Review and analyzes payroll process in order to recommend and implement changes leading to best-practice operations
Drive innovative solutions related to process, operations, and policy to leverage payroll services more fully.
Assess and provides resolutions to escalated payroll issues and incidents
Ensure global alignment in processes and measures through collaboration with other regions.
Provide orientation and on-going training for staff to ensure policies and procedures are followed.
Other Responsibilities
Assist in the definition of project scope and objectives, involving all relevant stakeholders and ensuring technical feasibility
Ensure resource availability and allocation
Support in the development of project plans to monitor and track progress
Develop project dashboards to report on status of initiatives. Report and escalate to management as needed
Manage the relationship with the client and all stakeholders
Establish and maintain relationships with third parties/vendors
Skills & Experience:
5 to 6 years-experience in Payroll 2 years of which was spent in a management capacity. An added advantage if the candidate has relevant experience in foreign capital company.
Must be a graduate of a bachelor’s degree. Accounting and HR related courses are preferred.
Knowledgeable on Japan tax, statutory and labor regulations. Prior experience in handling regional payroll is also advantage.
Good communication/business level English. Have good presentation, and facilitation skills.
With excellent skills in using Microsoft applications (Advance Excel, Macro, PowerPoint, etc)
With working knowledge in using Workday and an automated SAP payroll system. Prior experience is using ADP GlobalView system is an advantage.
Fast learner, detail-oriented and can work collaboratively within and outside other HR functions
Internal Guidelines about Work from Home:
Hybrid Model, following Manulife’s Working Better guidelines.
Over time:
Approx. 0-40 hours/month.
Salary:
Based on our Work Rules, we will consider your experience, skills and knowledge when we decide the salary.
Commutation allowance and Variable Incentive Bonus are paid based on our Work Rules.
Working Hours:
Standard Working Hours: 9:00am - 5:00pm (Include 1 hour break. Flex-time system available)
Benefits:
Social Insurance (Health Insurance, Employees' Pension, Unemployment Insurance, Worker's Accident Insurance), Benefit One, Training system (include Japanese lesson / English lesson), Childcare Leave/Family care Leave, Baby-sitter support program, wiwiw System (Support program for those who take Childcare Leave), Retirement Pension (only for Regular employee).
Holidays/Vacations:
Saturdays and Sundays
National Holidays
Year-end and new year holidays (December 30, December 31, January 2 and January 3)
Annual Paid Holiday
For Regular employee, other special holidays are available (e.g. Anniversary Leave, etc.)
Location:
Hatsudai Head Office, Tokyo Opera City, 3-20-2 Nishi Shinjuku, Shinjuku-ku, Tokyo Japan
What motivates you?
You obsess about customers, listen, engage and act for their benefit
You think big, with curiosity to discover ways to use your agile mindset and enable business outcomes
You thrive in teams, and enjoy getting things done together
You take ownership and build solutions, focusing on what matters
You do what is right, work with integrity and speak up
You share your humanity, helping us build a diverse and inclusive work environment for everyone
Our commitment to you
Our mission; to be a part of making Decisions Easier and Lives Better
A leadership team dedicated to your growth and success
A bold ambition and set of goals to be a leader in driving transformation in our industry
Our best. Every day.
Learn more about opportunities with us at jobs.manulife.com
About Manulife and John Hancock
Manulife Financial Corporation is a leading international financial services provider, helping people make their decisions easier and lives better. To learn more about us, visit https://www.manulife.com/en/about/our-story.html.
Manulife is an Equal Opportunity Employer
At Manulife/John Hancock, we embrace our diversity. We strive to attract, develop and retain a workforce that is as diverse as the customers we serve and to foster an inclusive work environment that embraces the strength of cultures and individuals. We are committed to fair recruitment, retention, advancement and compensation, and we administer all of our practices and programs without discrimination on the basis of race, ancestry, place of origin, colour, ethnic origin, citizenship, religion or religious beliefs, creed, sex (including pregnancy and pregnancy-related conditions), sexual orientation, genetic characteristics, veteran status, gender identity, gender expression, age, marital status, family status, disability, or any other ground protected by applicable law.
It is our priority to remove barriers to provide equal access to employment. A Human Resources representative will work with applicants who request a reasonable accommodation during the application process. All information shared during the accommodation request process will be stored and used in a manner that is consistent with applicable laws and Manulife/John Hancock policies. To request a reasonable accommodation in the application process, contact recruitment@manulife.com.
Working Arrangement
Hybrid

item.title: Manulife: Assistant Manager Actuarial
item.description:
locations
Kuala Lumpur, Wilayah Persekutuan Kuala Lumpur
time type
Full time
posted on
Posted Today
time left to apply
End Date: May 31, 2025 (30+ days left to apply)
job requisition id
JR25040197
The opportunity
This position is accountable for the accurate and timely development, production and analysis of actuarial financial models and management information for IFRS17 and MFRS17 reporting. These form the basis for assessing the Company’s financial performance and position. This position shares accountabilities with other departments such as Finance for the accurate and timely development, production, and analysis of financial reports.
.
Responsibilities
For IFRS 17 / MFRS 17 and financial statement related reporting:
Management information submission
Driver of earnings analysis
Valuation control (booking control, inclusion control, reserve trend & movement analysis, spreadsheet control and annual spotcheck)
Annual basis change
Liaise with external and internal auditor
For other OSFI requirements and reporting
LICAT capital reporting and sensitivity
Par Adequacy Testing
Economic Capital Sensitivity
Appointed Actuary Report
Experience monitoring and studies
Maintain and improve documentation of tasks for general reference
Initiate process improvements and streamline tasks
Other regional analysis and submissions as required
Provide support for other actuarial related matters and ad-hoc projects/initiatives from time to time
What motivates you?
You obsess about customers, listen, engage and act for their benefit.
You think big, with curiosity to discover ways to use your agile approach and enable business outcomes.
You thrive in teams and enjoy getting things done together.
You take ownership and build solutions, focusing on what matters.
You do what is right, work with integrity and speak up.
You share your humanity, helping us build a diverse and inclusive work environment for everyone.

What we are looking for
Preferably 3-5 years relevant experience.
Degree in Actuarial Science, Statistics, and Mathematics.
Good progress in actuarial professional exams.
Good time management and analytical skills.
Demonstrate ability to apply actuarial theories to develop practical solutions to various assignments and projects.
Good interpersonal skill
What can we offer you?
A competitive salary and benefits packages.
A growth trajectory that extends upward and outward, encouraging you to follow your passions and learn new skills.
A focus on growing your career path with us.
Flexible work policies and strong work-life balance.
Professional development and leadership opportunities.

Our commitment to you
Values-first culture
We lead with our Values every day and bring them to life together.
Boundless opportunity
We create opportunities to learn and grow at every stage of your career.
Continuous innovation
We invite you to help redefine the future of financial services.
Delivering the promise of Diversity, Equity and Inclusion
We foster an inclusive workplace where everyone thrives.
Championing Corporate Citizenship
We build a business that benefits all stakeholders and has a positive social and environmental impact.
About Manulife and John Hancock
Manulife Financial Corporation is a leading international financial services provider, helping people make their decisions easier and lives better. To learn more about us, visit https://www.manulife.com/en/about/our-story.html.
Manulife is an Equal Opportunity Employer
At Manulife/John Hancock, we embrace our diversity. We strive to attract, develop and retain a workforce that is as diverse as the customers we serve and to foster an inclusive work environment that embraces the strength of cultures and individuals. We are committed to fair recruitment, retention, advancement and compensation, and we administer all of our practices and programs without discrimination on the basis of race, ancestry, place of origin, colour, ethnic origin, citizenship, religion or religious beliefs, creed, sex (including pregnancy and pregnancy-related conditions), sexual orientation, genetic characteristics, veteran status, gender identity, gender expression, age, marital status, family status, disability, or any other ground protected by applicable law.
It is our priority to remove barriers to provide equal access to employment. A Human Resources representative will work with applicants who request a reasonable accommodation during the application process. All information shared during the accommodation request process will be stored and used in a manner that is consistent with applicable laws and Manulife/John Hancock policies. To request a reasonable accommodation in the application process, contact recruitment@manulife.com.

item.title: Manulife: Quality Engineer
item.description:
locations
Jakarta, Indonesia HO
time type
Full time
posted on
Posted Today
time left to apply
End Date: April 25, 2025 (5 days left to apply)
job requisition id
JR24091822
Responsibilities:
Collaborate with product owners and developers to analyze features in the applications under test.
Develop regression, smoke, and sanity test suites based on requirements or user stories.
Ensure the quality of testing deliverables for assigned projects.
Coordinate with application users to gather feedback.
Take ownership of the quality of applications and products to be delivered.
Design and implement automated test scripts and frameworks.
Conduct manual testing when necessary to ensure comprehensive coverage.
Monitor and report on test execution and defects.
Participate in code reviews and provide feedback from a testing perspective.
Continuously improve testing processes and methodologies.
Mentor and guide junior team members in best practices for testing and automation.
Requirements:
Minimum 5 years of hands-on experience in software testing.
Proficiency in at least one programming language.
Experience with Selenium, Appium, or equivalent automation tools.
Experience in API functional and performance testing.
Ability to create proper test scenarios for various testing purposes.
Proficiency in reporting and following up on defects.
Experience with Jira or similar collaboration tools.
Understanding of the software development life cycle.
Familiarity with agile and waterfall methodologies.
Proficiency in English communication.
Preferably, experience in the insurance industry.
About Manulife and John Hancock
Manulife Financial Corporation is a leading international financial services provider, helping people make their decisions easier and lives better. To learn more about us, visit https://www.manulife.com/en/about/our-story.html.
Manulife is an Equal Opportunity Employer
At Manulife/John Hancock, we embrace our diversity. We strive to attract, develop and retain a workforce that is as diverse as the customers we serve and to foster an inclusive work environment that embraces the strength of cultures and individuals. We are committed to fair recruitment, retention, advancement and compensation, and we administer all of our practices and programs without discrimination on the basis of race, ancestry, place of origin, colour, ethnic origin, citizenship, religion or religious beliefs, creed, sex (including pregnancy and pregnancy-related conditions), sexual orientation, genetic characteristics, veteran status, gender identity, gender expression, age, marital status, family status, disability, or any other ground protected by applicable law.

item.title: Manulife: Data Scientist
item.description:
locations
Chengdu, Sichuan
time type
Full time
posted on
Posted Today
time left to apply
End Date: April 30, 2025 (10 days left to apply)
job requisition id
JR25040653
Manulife is a leading international financial services provider, helping people make decisions easier and lives better. Help shape the future you want to see — and discover that better can take you anywhere you want to go.

JOB DESCRIPTION

Responsibilities:

• Develops and implements analytics enabled solutions to improve business process, to generate insights, support business goals and strategy development
• Owns and delivers complete projects of moderate scope and manages assigned tasks on more complex projects.
• Works on problems of moderate scope / complexity where analysis of situations or data requires a review of a variety of factors
• Ability to generate relevant, actionable insights based on iterative data analysis, translate data-driven output into business language, and to make appropriate recommendations to respective business stakeholders
• Provides analysis on datasets of significant complexity.
• Quickly able to develop enough business insight to connect data sources across multiple diverse and complicated systems.
• Communicates results of cases of moderate complexity to business partners and recommends solutions to more senior data scientists .
• Works closely with subject matter experts to understand data sources and business processes.
• Has intimate understanding of the business value chain and customer lifecycle for the projects they are supporting.
• Has fundamental understanding of the business as a whole.
• Curious and asks the right kinds of questions of peers and customers to gain knowledge.
• Understands company culture, cooperation & information sharing within the company
• Establishes an internal network and leverages the network of more senior data scientists.
• Develop an external network.
• Actively seeks out coaching and incorporates feedback into their work.
• Provides guidance and mentorship to more junior data scientists.
• Owns their career development.
• Takes advantage of available resources to advance their technical and business skills.
• Understands how their project supports broader business strategy, including the core drivers of business value

Some ability to construct systematic process to implement analytics insights and solutions, this includes (but not limited to):
1) designing a process with multiple relevant parties with clear specifications of roles and responsibilities
2) identifying required tasks, system / data flow and contingency
3) specifying check-and-balance throughout the process
4) establishing rigorous tracking and closed-loop learning
5) implement such process in a scalable way

Requirements:

• Advanced degree in Statistics, Math, Computer Science, or Engineering; or Bachelor’s degree with equivalent technical experience.
• Mnimum of 2 years of applicable experience
• Advanced knowledge of programming languages and concepts ( SAS, SPSS, R (different modules) or Python)
• Familiarity and working knowledge of basic and advanced statistical tests, distributions, maximum likelihood estimators, statistical modelling techniques (regression, cluster / factor / principle components, decision tree, survival analysis, etc.)
• Working knowledge of different techniques and knowledge of machine learning algorithms.
• Basic knowledge of AI toolkits.
• Advanced knowledge of data visualization tools such as Tableau, QlikView, open source data visualization libraries - ggplot and d3.
• Advanced knowledge of common database querying languages such as SQL.
• Capability of applying a systematic, structured approach to explore / navigate / mine massive amount of data, structured or un-structured, to uncover hidden patterns / insights.
• Strong working knowledge of relational database models ( data vaulting techniques, rules tables).


At Manulife/John Hancock, we embrace our diversity. We strive to attract, develop and retain a workforce that is as diverse as the customers we serve and to foster an inclusive work environment that embraces the strength of cultures and individuals. We are committed to fair recruitment, retention, advancement and compensation, and we administer all of our practices and programs without discrimination on the basis of race, ancestry, place of origin, colour, ethnic origin, citizenship, religion or religious beliefs, creed, sex (including pregnancy and pregnancy-related conditions), sexual orientation, genetic characteristics, veteran status, gender identity, gender expression, age, marital status, family status, disability, or any other ground protected by applicable law.

This job description is not a comprehensive listing of all job duties required for this role. We reserve the right to change these duties or assign additional duties at any time with or without notice.
About Manulife and John Hancock
Manulife Financial Corporation is a leading international financial services provider, helping people make their decisions easier and lives better. To learn more about us, visit https://www.manulife.com/en/about/our-story.html.
Manulife is an Equal Opportunity Employer
At Manulife/John Hancock, we embrace our diversity. We strive to attract, develop and retain a workforce that is as diverse as the customers we serve and to foster an inclusive work environment that embraces the strength of cultures and individuals. We are committed to fair recruitment, retention, advancement and compensation, and we administer all of our practices and programs without discrimination on the basis of race, ancestry, place of origin, colour, ethnic origin, citizenship, religion or religious beliefs, creed, sex (including pregnancy and pregnancy-related conditions), sexual orientation, genetic characteristics, veteran status, gender identity, gender expression, age, marital status, family status, disability, or any other ground protected by applicable law.

item.title: Manulife: GA Financing Specialist
item.description:
locations
Jakarta
time type
Full time
posted on
Posted Today
time left to apply
End Date: May 18, 2025 (28 days left to apply)
job requisition id
JR25030421
This position is responsible to manage GA Financing Specialist
Position Responsibilities:
Present Manulife in the most appropriate manner, including to introduce our Management, Products, and Operational processes.
Create and Manage Onboarding Schedules: Develop and oversee the onboarding schedule for new GA Owners. Make sure GA Owner, Leaders, Agents, and Admin will have a proper knowledge during their first months in Manulife.
Ensure that new GA Owners fully comprehend their Goals in Manulife and how to achieve it. This goal should be include their business performance and business quality metrics.
Coordinate with Relevant Departments: Collaborate with various departments, including Recruitment, Training, Operations, Line1B, and Compliance, to ensure that the new GA team receives proper knowledge transfer and understands Manulife's values.
Review and Improve Onboarding Programs: Assess the effectiveness of the onboarding program and make improvements as needed.
Identify and Resolve Onboarding Issues: Identify obstacles and problems within the onboarding program and implement solutions.
Ensure GA Office setup including Office Location, Office & Signage Renovation, KPM Contract, are properly and timely managed.
Required Qualifications:
Min. Bachelor Degree from Business Management / Data Analytic / or related major
Minimum 5 year in Insurance Industry
Experienced as an Life Insurance Agency Trainer
Knowledge in Insurance Agency Business Process
Windows and office
Strong verbal and written skills in Bahasa Indonesia
Strong inter-personal skills in English would be an asset
When you join our team:
We’ll empower you to learn and grow the career you want.
We’ll recognize and support you in a flexible environment where well-being and inclusion are more than just words.
As part of our global team, we’ll support you in shaping the future you want to see.
About Manulife and John Hancock
Manulife Financial Corporation is a leading international financial services provider, helping people make their decisions easier and lives better. To learn more about us, visit https://www.manulife.com/en/about/our-story.html.

We strive to attract, develop and retain a workforce that is as diverse as the customers we serve and to foster an inclusive work environment that embraces the strength of cultures and individuals. item.title: Manulife: Regional Agency Trainer
item.description:
locations
Jakarta
time type
Full time
posted on
Posted Today
time left to apply
End Date: May 18, 2025 (28 days left to apply)
job requisition id
JR25030029
Position Responsibilities:
• Support the agency to achieve sales targets every year.
• Achieve Sales Target (Sales Activities Penetration , Agency Activation)
• Increase Manpower through Creative Recruitment Activities (BOS, NEXT etc)
• Development Team Agency ( Evaluation , Training , Promotion Class )
• Develop Core Agents ( Increase SFP , MDRT )
• Drive Agency Initiatives
• Maintain and Develop Quality business (Reinstate , Legal and Compliance)
• Personal Development (Mastery Agency Compensation, Career, Reward and Product
• Measuring and Reporting
Required Qualifications:
• Minimum Bachelor-Degree
• Minimum of 5 years as Agency Business Development
• Good communication skills, positive thinking and able to solve agency problems, able to motivate, conduct training and make presentation materials properly and correctly
• Have an experience in leading a team
• English proficiency
• Ability to build and manage relationships with the key internal stakeholders
When you join our team:
We’ll empower you to learn and grow the career you want.

item.title: Manulife: Agency Strategy Manager – Life Insurance Industry
item.description:
locations
Jakarta
time type
Full time
posted on
Posted Today
time left to apply
End Date: May 18, 2025 (28 days left to apply)
job requisition id
JR25031047
Are you currently in a research or strategy role at management consulting firm and looking for an opportunity to apply your problem-solving skills in a fast-growing industry? Our Agency Strategy team is looking for a highly analytical and strategic thinker to help drive transformation in one of Indonesia’s largest life insurance distribution channels.
What You’ll Do:
Analyze and enhance agency strategy, including branch cohort planning, GA segmentation, and activity management initiatives.
Conduct deep-dive research and data analysis on agency productivity, market trends, and competitor strategies.
Lead and prepare structured business reviews with top management, ensuring strategic alignment across teams.
Collaborate with cross-functional teams, including sales, product, and finance, to execute strategic initiatives effectively.
Work closely with regional and global stakeholders to align local strategy with best practices and corporate priorities.
What We’re Looking For:
2-4 years of experience in a research, strategy, or consulting role (preferably from BCG, Bain, McKinsey, or a similar environment).
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills – ability to translate data into actionable insights.
Experience in business planning, reporting, and stakeholder management.
Ability to work in a fast-paced, high-stakes environment and communicate effectively with senior leaders.
Interest in financial services and distribution strategy is a plus, but industry experience is not required.
Why Join Us?
Direct exposure to senior leadership and strategic decision-making in one of Indonesia’s largest insurance companies.
Opportunity to shape the future of agency distribution through data-driven strategy and innovation.
A dynamic, high-impact role where your insights will directly drive business performance.
Interested? Let’s connect! Send your CV to [your email] with the subject: Application – Agency Strategy Manager.
About Manulife and John Hancock
Manulife Financial Corporation is a leading international financial services provider, helping people make their decisions easier and lives better. To learn more about us, visit https://www.manulife.com/en/about/our-story.html.
Manulife is an Equal Opportunity Employer
At Manulife/John Hancock, we embrace our diversity. We strive to attract, develop and retain a workforce that is as diverse as the customers we serve and to foster an inclusive work environment that embraces the strength of cultures and individuals. We are committed to fair recruitment, retention, advancement and compensation, and we administer all of our practices and programs without discrimination on the basis of race, ancestry, place of origin, colour, ethnic origin, citizenship, religion or religious beliefs, creed, sex (including pregnancy and pregnancy-related conditions), sexual orientation, genetic characteristics, veteran status, gender identity, gender expression, age, marital status, family status, disability, or any other ground protected by applicable law.
It is our priority to remove barriers to provide equal access to employment. A Human Resources representative will work with applicants who request a reasonable accommodation during the application process. All information shared during the accommodation request process will be stored and used in a manner that is consistent with applicable laws and Manulife/John Hancock policies. To request a reasonable accommodation in the application process, contact recruitment@manulife.com.
Working Arrangement
Hybrid

item.title: Manulife: Accounting
item.description:
locations
Jakarta
time type
Full time
posted on
Posted Today
time left to apply
End Date: May 18, 2025 (28 days left to apply)
job requisition id
JR25010464
Are you looking for a supportive, collaborative workplace with great teams and inspiring leaders? You’ve come to the right place. We’re looking for ambitious people who share our values and want to make every day better for people around the world. If this sounds like you, and the career below sounds exciting, we’d like to hear from you.
The Opportunity
The customer is the focus of everything we do, and millions of end users rely on our products daily. We believe in the value of empowering Bancassurance Analyst with the resources to solve critical problems for the future of our business, which is why we need you.
What motivates you?
You obsess about customers, listen, engage and act for their benefit
You think big, with curiosity to discover ways to use your agile mindset and enable business outcomes
You thrive in teams, and enjoy getting things done together
You take ownership and build solutions, focusing on what matters
You do what is right, work with integrity and speak up
You share your humanity, helping us build a diverse and inclusive work environment for everyone
We are looking for someone with:
The main role is responsible for verification and preparation of Group Life Health journal entries & Sharia journal entries, tracking customer requests/problems to ensure resolution.
The others role is responsible in Accounting Filling Management Proccess, Access Review of Accounting shared folder, BUCP Recovery team support, Finance BUPC Coordinator and user admin in AJMI’s Bank Partner System.
Keep customer informed especially if delays/problems are encountered. Looks beyond customers’ immediate request and offers other helpful information.
The role necessarily needs a good relationship with the each team member and other departments in Manulife Indonesia, external auditors and regulator’s person in charge.
Nice to Haves:
Maintain relationship with internal (AJMI’s GLH, AJMI’s Group Saving Operations, AJMI’s Sharia Operations, Treasury, Tax, Compliance team and Accounting Team Member) and external (public accounting auditors, government officials, and peers in other member companies).
On the job you will:
1. Prepare AJMI’s GLH & Sharia Manual journal posted in accurate and timely manner:
Prepared AJMI’s GLH and Sharia accrual & adjustment manual journal, include daily (if any), weekly (if any) and monthly (especially month end) journal entries:
Prepare weekly CB journal to Reclass CAS4G transaction from Account 800000 to 800605
Upload Monthly CBPayments of Buffer Funds & Customer Loading.
Upload Monthly CBReceipt of Cashback Hospital that receive from MBPS team
Prepare Monthly JETS Reclass journal AU from CAS4G transactions
Prepare Quarterly JETS Accrual journal Lost Waskita
Prepare JETS Reclass & Adjustment journal from Bank Reconciliation & BS Reconciliation.
Upload Monthly JETS Reclass journal of Refund CRV GLH 947003
Prepare Monthly JETS Adjustment Manual Sharia OJK Claim PF950500
Prepare Monthly JETS Reclass journal from OldAcc 966052 to OldAcc 966053
Review Monthly JETS Reclass journal pf Premium In Advance (PIA) GLH from MBPS team.
Prepare NB & Renewal Premium Income CAS4G data & send it to GLH Ops team
Review Monthly JETS Accrual of Undo NB & Renewal Premium Income GLH from MBPS team
Prepare Monthly CBPayments & CBReceipts of Bank Charges & Interest GLH
Review Monthly JETS Accrual journal of Premi Adira Konven from MBPS team.
Review Monthly JETS Accrual journal of Premi Adira Sharia from MBPS team.
Review Monthly JETS journal of Commissions & Bank Commissions from MBPS team
Review Monthly JETS Accrual journal of Outstanding Claim GLH & CL from MBPS team
Review Monthly JETS Local Accrual journal of Outstanding Claim GLH & CL from MBPS team
Review Monthly JETS Reclass Sharia Journal of Double Claim BHS from MBPS team
Prepare extract update data client GLH & GS
Review Monthly JETS Reclass journal of Blank Distribution Channel in Konven CAS4G transaction from MBPS team.
Review Monthly JETS Reclass journal of Blank Distribution Channel in Sharia CAS4G transaction from MBPS team.
Review Monthly JETS Reclass journal of Blank Distribution Channel in Accrual Outstanding Claim GLH from MBPS team.
Review Monthly JETS journal of Provision GLH from MBPS team.
Review Monthly JETS Reclass journal Sharia of Ujrah Zafirah Medcash from MBPS team.
Review Monthly JETS journal Sharia of Commissions & DAC
Review Monthly JETS journal Sharia of CTA Ujrah & Commissions
Ensure all accrual & adjustment transactions in AJMI’s GLH and Sharia have recorded in LAWSON System.
Verify AJMI’s GLH daily transaction feed from CAS4G to LAWSON system:
Responsible to review daily reconciliation and analyzing data feed transaction CAS4G from MBPS team.
Ensure all daily feed transactions journal CAS4G uploaded in LAWSON System.
Prepare AJMI’s CAS4G transaction feed from Cashbook to LAWSON system:
Responsible to prepare daily reconciliation and daily analyzing data CAS4G transaction in CashBook.
Responsible to prepare & upload CBPayment & CBReceipt
Ensure all daily feed Cashbook transactions journal uploaded in LAWSON System.
Prepare & Review AJMI’s Group Monthly, Quarterly & Others Report:
Prepare Monthly Bank Reconciliation & BS Reconciliation
Review Monthly Outstanding Claim Group report for Actuary that prepare by MBPS team.
Review Monthly Working Paper Accrual Claim Group Credit Life report for Actuary team that prepare by MBPS team.
Prepare Monthly Premium Income Health Life Group report for Actuary team.
Review Monthly Premium Breakdown Group report for Actuary team that prepare by MBPS team.
Review Monthly Claim Breakdown Group report for Actuary team that prepare by MBPS team.
Prepare Monthly OJK Aging Receivable (rincian L-1 (201)) Group report for Local Reporting team.
Prepare Monthly OJK Utang Claim Group report for Local Reporting team
Prepare Monthly OJK Premium Group report by Kabupaten for Local Reporting team.
Prepare Monthly OJK Claim Group report by Kabupaten for Local Reporting team.
Prepare Monthly Customer Loading report for Group Billing team & MBPS team
Prepare Quarterly Suspense Group report for Regional Office team.
Prepare Quarterly OJK Premium Group report by Business Nature for Local Reporting team.
Prepare Quarterly OJK Claim Group report by Business Nature for Local Reporting team.
Prepare Monthly Sharia Data Ujrah for Sharia Operations Team
Prepare Monthly Sharia Data Commission Allocation fro Sharia Operations Team
Prepare data & report request for Internal & External Audit team.
Others:
Responsible in Accounting Filling Management.
Responsible to check & review mature Accounting document (10+1 years) before destroyed it.
Responsible in managing & review access user in shared folder Accounting and Sharepoint Indonesia Accounting
Responsible as an Admin in AJMI’s Internet Bank Partner System in Manulife Indonesia.
Responsible to prepared and send information of Monthly BI Midrate.
Responsible in Accounting BUCP recovery team support.
Responsible in Finance Business Units Privacy Coordinator.
Personal Development
Adopt AJMI culture of professionalism, integrity, effectiveness, dynamic, and customer centric attitude contributing to an internal environment of teamwork and promote positive brand image to Customers Undertake relevant training and personal development course as mapped by HR Department Comply with any specific responsibilities necessary as outlined by HR Department and ensure keep up to date with developments in these areas
Continuous Improvement
Support special project (on requirement basis) related to AJMI’s GLH Accounting.
Measuring and Reporting
Report to: Head of Pension & Group Accounting

Our mission; to be a part of making Decisions Easier and Lives Better
A leadership team dedicated to your growth and success
A bold ambition and set of goals to be a leader in driving transformation in our industry
Our best. Every day.

About Manulife and John Hancock
Manulife Financial Corporation is a leading international financial services provider, helping people make their decisions easier and lives better. To learn more about us, visit https://www.manulife.com/en/about/our-story.html.
Manulife is an Equal Opportunity Employer
At Manulife/John Hancock, we embrace our diversity. We strive to attract, develop and retain a workforce that is as diverse as the customers we serve and to foster an inclusive work environment that embraces the strength of cultures and individuals. We are committed to fair recruitment, retention, advancement and compensation, and we administer all of our practices and programs without discrimination on the basis of race, ancestry, place of origin, colour, ethnic origin, citizenship, religion or religious beliefs, creed, sex (including pregnancy and pregnancy-related conditions), sexual orientation, genetic characteristics, veteran status, gender identity, gender expression, age, marital status, family status, disability, or any other ground protected by applicable law.
It is our priority to remove barriers to provide equal access to employment. A Human Resources representative will work with applicants who request a reasonable accommodation during the application process. All information shared during the accommodation request process will be stored and used in a manner that is consistent with applicable laws and Manulife/John Hancock policies. To request a reasonable accommodation in the application process, contact recruitment@manulife.com.
Working Arrangement
Hybrid
